what_it_does: Defines a React-style action toolbar with icon buttons for select, filter, sort, clear filters, create, and upload, plus a controlled search input, then assigns the toolbar component to window.ActionToolbar.
observations: Uses a small local SVG helper to render icons from path data arrays.
Toolbar buttons share cv-btn classes with primary or outline variants.
Select, Create New, and Upload controls are conditionally rendered through boolean props.
The search input is controlled by the search prop and reports changes through optional onSearch.
The component is exposed globally through window.ActionToolbar instead of an ES module export.
Icons.search is defined but the search field uses an inline SVG instead.
standouts: Compact file-local icon vocabulary for toolbar actions.
Toolbar layout includes a right-aligned group for create and upload actions.
Search is the only action with a callback wired through ActionToolbar props.
conventions: CSS class names use a cv- prefix and modifier style cv-btn--primary/cv-btn--outline.
Optional behavior is handled with default prop values and optional chaining.
Inline SVGs use a shared ic class and a 14x14 viewBox.
concerns: ActionToolbar does not expose callbacks for select, filter, sort, clear, create, or upload buttons.
ToolbarBtn buttons omit an explicit type attribute.
The I helper sets fill but does not set stroke, so line icons depend on external styling to appear as intended.
The search input relies on placeholder text and has no explicit accessible label in this file.
notes: A compact globally exposed toolbar component for common platform actions with a controlled search field.
questions: Should toolbar actions other than search receive callback props?
Should Icons.search replace the inline search SVG for consistency?
Should the primary variant be used by any rendered button in this file?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in the comment matches the rendered Select, Filter, Sort, Search, Clear, Create, and Upload controls.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a reusable platform toolbar pattern with common list or table actions and consistent button/icon styling.
novelty: The file combines a tiny path-array icon helper with a globally assigned component rather than a module export.
contribution: This file contributes a reusable action toolbar structure with shared button styling hooks, optional select/create/upload controls, a right-side action group, and a controlled search input.
summary_for_embedding: ActionToolbar.jsx defines a globally exposed React-style toolbar with SVG icon buttons for select, filter, sort, clear filters, create, upload, and a controlled search input.
intention_for_embedding: This was for presenting common platform actions in one compact, styled toolbar component.

symbols.does: Renders a 14x14 SVG icon from an array of path definitions.
symbols.description: I is a small icon helper that maps each string in d to a path element inside an svg with class ic. Its fill prop toggles path fill between currentColor and none, leaving other SVG styling to attributes or external CSS.

symbols.does: Renders a styled toolbar button with an icon, label, optional primary class, and click handler.
symbols.description: ToolbarBtn centralizes the button markup for toolbar actions. It chooses between cv-btn--primary and cv-btn--outline based on the primary prop and places the supplied icon before the label.

symbols.does: Renders the full toolbar with optional select/create/upload buttons, filter and sort buttons, a search input, clear filters, and a right action group.
symbols.description: ActionToolbar composes the toolbar from local icon definitions, ToolbarBtn instances, and a controlled search field. It defaults select, create, and upload visibility to true, defaults search to an empty string, and calls onSearch when the input value changes.
